title_en: Can Tennis Survive Its New Golden Age?
title_zh: 网球能否度过它的新黄金时代？
rubric_en: The next generation of elite players has arrived. On the court, they are delivering astonishing play. Off the court, they aren’t entirely happy.
rubric_zh: 新一代顶级球员已经登场。他们在场上打出惊人的表现；场下，却并非完全满意。

paragraphs.en: The golden age of tennis didn’t end with a whimper. After Roger Federer walked off the court for the final time, at the 2022 Laver Cup, he broke into full-on sobs. Federer’s doubles partner at that event in London, his longtime rival Rafael Nadal, seemed to understand that he was staring down his own tennis mortality as well. After the match, the two players, their faces streaked with tears, touched hands as they sat side by side. It felt at once like a private moment between intimates and a communal wake for a sport they’d helped define for decades.
paragraphs.zh: 网球的黄金时代并非在沉默中结束。罗杰·费德勒在 2022 年劳弗杯的最后一次退场时，当场放声大哭。费德勒在伦敦那场比赛中的搭档、他长期以来的对手拉法·纳达尔也似乎意识到，自己也正在面对职业生涯的尽头。赛后，两位选手面带泪痕并肩坐着，相视握手。那一刻既像两个知己之间的私人瞬间，也像是为一项他们数十年来共同塑造的运动举行的公众送别仪式。

paragraphs.en: Another of the game’s 21st-century icons also got weepy when she played at that year’s U.S. Open. In an essay for Vogue, Serena Williams confessed that she felt “no happiness” as she said farewell. Williams refused to use the word retirement, preferring to say that she was “evolving away from tennis.”
paragraphs.zh: 在 2020 年代，这项运动的另一位标志性人物在当年的美国网球公开赛上也同样泪流满面。在《Vogue》的一篇文章中，塞雷娜·威廉姆斯坦白说自己在告别时感到“没有快乐”。威廉姆斯拒绝使用“退役”这个词，转而说自己“正在远离网球，朝着另一种人生发展”。

paragraphs.en: As these champions left the scene, their sport clearly needed to evolve too. Tennis was entering a rebuilding phase during which the legends of the past would make way for … someone, hopefully, at some point. The roster at that Laver Cup, an all-star competition on the men’s circuit, was littered with hyped young players, such as Stefanos Tsitsipas, Félix Auger-Aliassime, and Taylor Fritz, who had yet to win one of tennis’s four major tournaments (the Australian Open, the French Open, Wimbledon, and the U.S. Open). At the 2022 U.S. Open, a pair of women seen as Williams’s potential heirs—the tournament’s previous two winners, the then-teenage phenom Emma Raducanu and the four-time major champion Naomi Osaka—each lost in the first round.
paragraphs.zh: 当这些冠军逐渐离开赛场时，网球显然也需要随之进化。该运动正进入重建阶段：过去的传奇们终将让位给……希望某个时间点出现的新人。那届劳弗杯——一场男单明星赛——的名单上，聚集了许多被热捧的年轻球员，如斯特法诺斯·齐西帕斯、费利克斯·奥热尔-阿利亚西姆和泰勒·弗里茨，他们当时都还没有赢得网球四大公开赛（澳大利亚网球公开赛、法国公开赛、温布尔登网球公开赛和美国网球公开赛）中的任何一项。与此同时，在 2022 年美国网球公开赛上，曾被视为威廉姆斯潜在接班人的两位女球员——当时拿下前两次冠军的“天才少女”艾玛·拉杜卡努和四次大满贯冠军拿破仑奥萨卡——都在首轮便告失利。

paragraphs.en: Tennis fans, it seemed, had no choice but to be patient. This next generation needed time to grow into consistent championship form. But in fact, tennis’s new wave was already crashing ashore.
paragraphs.zh: 这似乎说明网球迷别无选择，只能耐心等待。新一代需要时间成长为稳定的冠军争夺者。然而，事实上，网球的新潮汹涌地浪已经开始拍打上岸。

paragraphs.en: At that same U.S. Open, a couple of European neophytes—19-year-old Carlos Alcaraz and 21-year-old Jannik Sinner—slugged it out for a grueling five hours and 15 minutes before Alcaraz struck the final blow at 2:50 a.m. Their marathon quarterfinal featured a running behind-the-back forehand (by Alcaraz), a saved match point (Alcaraz again), and shot-making so absurd that it felt extraterrestrial. The following night, 21-year-old Iga Świątek and 24-year-old Aryna Sabalenka faced each other for the first time in a Grand Slam. In a semifinal match replete with punishing ground strokes and astonishing all-court defending, Świątek earned a comeback three-set victory.
paragraphs.zh: 就在同一届美国网球公开赛中，两位欧洲新秀——19岁的卡洛斯·阿尔卡拉斯和21岁的扬尼克·西纳——在一场消耗惨烈的五小时十五分钟大战后，于凌晨2:50分，阿尔卡拉斯打出最后一击。那场马拉松般的四分之一决赛中，阿尔卡拉斯打出了后背抢救式正手球，随后又拿下了赛点救球，整场比赛的击球质量离奇到让人感觉像外星人一样。第二天晚上，21岁的伊加·夏威特克与24岁的阿丽娜·萨巴伦卡首次在大满贯赛场上交锋。面对一场充满了沉重底线击球与惊人全场防守的半决赛，夏威特克赢得了一场三盘逆转胜。

paragraphs.en: Before that tournament, this tennis quartet had claimed two major titles, both won by Świątek. Less than four years later, they’re up to 21—seven for Alcaraz, six for Świątek, and four each for Sinner and Sabalenka. Only two other players have won more than a single major during this time: Novak Djokovic—the greatest male player ever, still battling the youngsters at age 39—and Coco Gauff, whose talent and drive have her in position to join tennis’s new pantheon.
paragraphs.zh: 在那场比赛之前，这四位球员已经拿下了两个大满贯冠军，都是夏威特克夺得的。不到四年后，他们的总冠军数已增至21座：阿尔卡拉斯7座、夏威特克6座、西纳和萨巴伦卡各4座。只有两位球员在此期间拿到过多于一座大满贯冠军：诺瓦克·德约科维奇——这位有史以来最伟大的男球员，39岁仍在与后辈们搏击；以及科科·高夫，她的天赋和进取心使她有望加入网球的新殿堂。

paragraphs.en: The rapid emergence of this cohort of stars is the inciting event in The Cruelest Game: Chasing Greatness in Professional Tennis, the journalist Matthew Futterman’s account of the thrilling and grueling lives of the tennis elite. Reaching the summit of a sport played by 100 million people worldwide is close to impossible. Staying at the top isn’t much easier. Even as these exhilarating talents have reenergized the game and risen above their peers, they’ve been brought low by injuries, doping scandals, and self-doubt. After Gauff lost in January’s Australian Open, she was caught on camera obliterating her racket in what she thought was a hidden corridor. She immediately bemoaned the breach of privacy, suggesting that “maybe some conversations can be had” about the burdens of life in the tennis panopticon. Świątek was less politic, asking in a press conference, “Are we tennis players, or are we animals in the zoo, where they’re observed even when they poop?”
paragraphs.zh: 这批新星的快速崛起正是马修·法特曼《The Cruelest Game: Chasing Greatness in Professional Tennis》一书的核心导火索。这本书记录了职业网球精英令人惊心动魄却又痛苦的生活。要在全球拥有1亿观众的运动中登顶，几乎是不可能完成的；而要在巅峰位置长期坚持，也并不轻松。即使这些令人振奋的天赋重振了比赛、超越了同龄对手，他们仍被伤病、兴奋剂丑闻和自我怀疑击垮。高夫在1月澳网失利后，她被拍到在一个她以为私密的走廊里砸坏球拍。她随后立刻抱怨被侵犯隐私，表示“也许我们可以谈谈”网球全景监视下的人生负担。夏威特克在发布会上的说法更直接：“我们是网球运动员，还是动物园里的动物，连拉屎都要被人观察？”

paragraphs.en: The tennis ecosystem has unexpectedly thrived in the absence of Federer, Nadal, and Williams, but the gains—for the sport and for its marquee players—are far from secure. Although Futterman adeptly surveys tennis’s stressors, the shape of the crisis that tennis faces, and its stakes, eludes him. Everything about the professional game—the way it’s played, structured, and consumed—seems ripe for transformation.
paragraphs.zh: 网球生态系统在费德勒、纳达尔和威廉姆斯退场后，竟然反而繁荣起来了；但这项运动和其头牌球员获得的收益远未稳固。尽管法特曼敏锐地梳理了网球中的压力源，以及网球所面临的危机形态与所涉赌注，但危机的具体轮廓在他笔下仍显模糊。职业网球的每一个方面——比赛本身的打法、结构安排以及消费方式——都似乎已经准备好迎接变革。

paragraphs.en: Futterman, who covers the pro circuit for The Athletic, wants us to understand that tennis is incredibly hard, perhaps especially for those who make it look easy. “There is no instruction manual,” he writes, “to help someone navigate their way through a sport and a culture seemingly designed to mess with their brains.”
paragraphs.zh: 《The Athletic》的职业赛场记者 Futterman 想让我们明白，网球非常难打，尤其是对那些看起来轻松的人来说更是如此。他写道：“这项运动和一种几乎是专门为了扰乱人脑而设计的文化，没有哪本说明书可以指引人如何应对。”

paragraphs.en: Much of that torment is intrinsic to the game. Every sport has moments of tension, but in tennis the anxiety is metronomic. The stress starts early—flinch on a break chance two minutes in and the whole first set might be gone—and it never really stops. On the court, there’s no such thing as running out the clock or calling for a sub.
paragraphs.zh: 这些折磨中有很大一部分是这项运动内在的一部分。每项运动都有紧张时刻，但在网球里，焦虑是有节奏的、不断敲打的。压力在很早就开始——第二分钟在一次破发机会上一个小小的迟疑，整第一盘可能就会丢掉——而且几乎从不停歇。上场比赛时，你不能像其他运动那样等时间耗尽，也不能像请求替补那样暂停。

paragraphs.en: Anger, isolation, and exhaustion are endemic to each tennis era, as much a part of the game as the fuzz on the balls. Just ask the perpetually tortured John McEnroe or his cooler-headed Scandinavian rival, Björn Borg, who packed up his rackets in his mid-20s and became, in Futterman’s view, “the canary in the coal mine” for tennis burnout. Another early-’80s sensation, the prodigy Andrea Jaeger, retired at 20 and later became a nun. World No. 1 Ashleigh Barty abruptly quit in 2022, saying simply, “I am spent.” And Andre Agassi, the sport’s bard of introspection, said this in his autobiography, Open: “I hate tennis, hate it with a dark and secret passion, and always have.”
paragraphs.zh: 愤怒、孤独和疲惫在每个网球时代都根深蒂固，是这项运动像球毛一样始终存在的一部分。只要问问长期饱受折磨的约翰·麦肯罗，或者他那冷静得多的斯堪的纳维亚对手博比·鲍格就知道了——后者在二十多岁就收起球拍，并在 Futterman 看来成了网球“职业倦怠”的“煤矿里的警报鸟”。另一位八十年代初的天才，安德烈·贾格，也在20岁时退役，后来成为修女。世界第一的阿什利·巴蒂在2022年突然宣布退役，只说了一句：“我已经筋疲力尽。”而网球界最富自省气质的安德烈·阿加西在自传《Open》里写道：“我讨厌网球，带着一种阴沉又隐秘的激情去讨厌它，而且我一向如此。”

paragraphs.en: At the risk of undercutting an all-time great: I love tennis. Plenty of players love it too—at least when they’re winning. (No one is more delighted by a beautiful Carlos Alcaraz drop shot than Carlos Alcaraz.) Whether I’m watching matches, listening to press conferences, or scrolling on Instagram, I’m struck that it’s never been better to be one of the best tennis players in the world. It’s also never been worse.
paragraphs.zh: 有点打破对顶级球员的神话，我爱网球。很多球员也爱它——至少在他们赢球的时候。（没人会比卡洛斯·阿尔卡拉斯更喜欢自己漂亮的吊球了，不也是卡洛斯·阿尔卡拉斯。）无论我是在看比赛、听新闻发布会，还是在刷 Instagram，我都会被一点事实打动：要成为世界上最好的网球选手，从未像现在这样容易；但也从未像现在这样艰难。

paragraphs.en: Read: Tennis explains everything
paragraphs.zh: 相关阅读：网球无所不包

paragraphs.en: Financially speaking, this is the sport’s golden era. Borg, an 11-time major champion, collected $3.6 million in career prize money. Alcaraz and Sabalenka won $5 million each for their victorious runs at the 2025 U.S. Open. For women especially, the allure of professional tennis is easy to quantify. Forbes’s 2025 list of the world’s highest-paid female athletes features Gauff in the No. 1 spot, with $33 million in on- and off-court income. She’s followed immediately by Sabalenka ($30 million), Świątek ($25 million), and six more tennis players in the top 12. (The leading basketball player on the list, Caitlin Clark, is No. 11, sandwiched between American tennis stars Jessica Pegula and Amanda Anisimova.)
paragraphs.zh: 从财务角度看，这的确是这项运动的黄金时代。11 次大满贯冠军的博格职业生涯奖金收入为 360 万美元。阿尔卡拉斯和萨巴伦卡在 2025 年美国公开赛夺冠后，各自赢得了 500 万美元。对女性来说，职业网球的吸引力尤其容易量化。富比士（Forbes）2025 年世界最高薪女性运动员榜单中，戈夫位居第一，场内外总收入为 3300 万美元。她之后紧跟着是萨巴伦卡（3000 万美元）、希瓦泰克（2500 万美元），以及另外 6 名入选前 12 名的网球选手。（榜单中排名第一的篮球运动员凯特琳·克拉克位居第 11 名，刚好排在美国网球名将杰西卡·皮古拉和阿曼达·阿尼西莫娃之间。）

paragraphs.en: Tennis players are essentially their own small business, with ever-expanding retinues—not just agents, managers, coaches, and trainers, but dietary advisers and sports psychologists to tune up their bodies and minds. Futterman gets his best material from these support staffers. Sabalenka’s “high-performance coach,” Jason Stacy, a practitioner of Brazilian jiu-jitsu who professes to “know nothing about tennis,” taught her breathing exercises that, according to Futterman, he “used to follow when an opponent would have him in an upside-down headlock.” (He also called in a biomechanics expert to fix her balky serve; that same specialist now works with Gauff.)
paragraphs.zh: 网球运动员本质上就是自己的小型公司，身边的随行团队也在不断扩张——不仅有经纪人、经理、教练和体能教练，还包括营养顾问和运动心理学家，用来调校他们的身体和心态。福特曼（Futterman）的最佳素材正是来自这些支援人员。萨巴伦卡的“高表现力教练”杰森·斯泰西（Jason Stacy）是一位巴西柔术练习者，他声称“对网球一无所知”，却教她进行呼吸训练；据福特曼介绍，这种训练曾帮助他“在对手给了他一个倒挂颈锁时保持控制”。（他还请来一位生物力学专家来修正她笨拙的发球；那位专家如今也在帮助戈夫。）

paragraphs.en: Deep insights from the athletes themselves, in The Cruelest Game and in press coverage generally, are scant. Top players now cultivate their image primarily via social media, affording meaningful (and not so meaningful) access only to their own videographers. A recent “unfiltered” Q&A on Sinner’s personal YouTube channel featured such questions as “How many hours do you usually sleep?” and “Which shoe do you tie first?” The three-month ban that Sinner served last year after testing positive for an anabolic steroid did not come up. (Tennis’s anti-doping authorities ruled that Sinner was not at fault, accepting his explanation that trace amounts of the drug had been rubbed into his body during a massage. Świątek, too, got a finding of “no significant fault” and a light suspension when she tested positive for the banned substance trimetazidine in 2024.)
paragraphs.zh: 从运动员本人出发的深度观点，无论在《The Cruelest Game》还是媒体采访中，都非常有限。如今顶级选手主要通过社交媒体打造个人形象，这让记者能接触到的内容大多只是由他们自己的摄像团队提供的，真正有分量的访问和信息很少。辛纳在个人 YouTube 频道发布的一场近期“未剪辑”问答，出现过“你通常睡几个小时？”、“你先系哪只鞋？”这样的提问。辛纳去年因检测出合成类固醇阳性后服过的三个月停赛并未被提及。（网球反兴奋剂机构裁定辛纳无过失，接受了他关于药物痕量可能在按摩过程中被涂到体表的解释。希瓦泰克在 2024 年检出禁用药物替米他嗪后，也被认定“无重大过错”，并被给予了较轻的禁赛处罚。）

paragraphs.en: Friendly YouTube Q&As notwithstanding, life online can be perilous for players, especially women. Tennis has become a hotbed of online harassment, a phenomenon fueled by omnipresent legalized gambling. Thanks to the sport’s international reach, its huge inventory of matches, and its continual discrete action—FanDuel and DraftKings both accept wagers on individual points—tennis is particularly popular with bettors worldwide, ranking behind only soccer and basketball by some estimates. For years now, high-profile women on tour have been speaking out about the torrent of sexualized and violent threats they receive from angry gamblers. Still, nothing seems to change. This past March, two women reported getting texts demanding that they lose a match; those messages were accompanied by a photo of a gun and the names of their family members.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管YouTube上的友好问答依旧如常，但对球员而言，尤其是对女性来说，网络生活仍然充满风险。网球已成为网络骚扰的温床，而这种现象在无处不在且“合法化”的博彩环境下愈发严重。得益于这项运动的国际化覆盖、庞大的比赛场次库存，以及持续不断的实时赛点特性——FanDuel和DraftKings都允许对单个球分进行下注——根据一些估算，网球在全球投注人群中的流行程度仅次于足球和篮球。多年来，巡回赛上的知名女性球员一直在发声，指出她们不断收到来自愤怒赌客的性化和暴力威胁。尽管如此，情况似乎并未改变。今年3月，曾有两位女性球员报告收到短信，要求她们输掉一场比赛；这些信息还附带了枪支照片以及她们家庭成员的名字。

paragraphs.en: Although this variety of cruelty goes unaddressed in The Cruelest Game, Futterman does highlight the unprecedented physical toll of tennis circa 2026. Thanks to modern fitness regimes and equipment—high-tech rackets and strings that make any shot possible from anywhere on the court—the sport is far more demanding than it was in Agassi’s day, let alone McEnroe and Borg’s. From the couch or the stands, high-level tennis can appear weightless. At ground level, it’s a brutal struggle, an hours-long series of leg-churning, lung-bursting sprints, punctuated by torquing, full-body whips of the racket, often in close-to-boiling temperatures. (Sinner’s two toughest opponents in recent years have been Alcaraz and the sun.) That ordeal has a cost: strained muscles, twisted knees, stretched and torn ligaments.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管这种残忍现象在《The Cruelest Game》里并未被提及，富特曼却指出了2026年前后网球前所未有的身体负荷。得益于现代化的体能训练体系以及高科技球拍和球线，球员几乎可以在球场任何位置打出任何球，网球比安加西时代更具挑战性，更别提麦肯罗与博格时代了。坐在沙发上看，或在看台上观战时，高水平网球似乎像无重力运动；但在场上，它是残酷的对抗，是长达数小时的腿部翻卷、肺部憋爆般的冲刺，再加上持续性的整身用力甩拍，且常发生在接近沸腾的高温中。（近几年，辛纳面临的两位最难对手就是阿尔卡拉斯和烈日。）这种折磨有代价：肌肉拉伤、膝关节扭伤、韧带拉伸过度乃至撕裂。

paragraphs.en: From the January 1938 issue: Inside tennis
paragraphs.zh: 摘自1938年1月刊：网球

paragraphs.en: Tennis isn’t just getting tougher from shot to shot. The tour itself is a multinational grind, with key events in Australia, the Middle East, North America, Europe, and East Asia. Tennis also has a shockingly short offseason—about a month for highly ranked players. The superstars aren’t happy. Sabalenka said earlier this year that the schedule “is definitely insane, and that’s not good for all of us, as you see so many players getting injured.” In 2024, Alcaraz said with a resigned chuckle, “Probably they are going to kill us in some way.” A year later, he pleaded with tennis authorities “to do something,” because there’s no “good period of time that we can practice, we can rest.” This year, he finally got some extended time off: He missed the French Open and Wimbledon because of an injured right wrist.
paragraphs.zh: 网球的艰难并不只体现在一球一球之间。巡回赛本身就是一场多国奔波的磨炼，重点赛事遍布澳大利亚、中东、北美、欧洲和东亚。网球赛季的休整期也短得惊人——对高排名球员来说，大约只有一个月。巨星们并不满意。萨巴连卡今年早些时候说，赛程“确实疯狂，这对我们所有人都不好，你会看到那么多球员受伤。”2024年，阿尔卡拉斯带着无奈的笑意说：“他们可能会以某种方式把我们弄残。”一年后，他向网球管理机构恳请“采取一些行动”，因为球员没有“一段可以好好训练、好好休息的时间”。今年，他终于获得了一段较长的休息：由于右手腕受伤，他错过了法国网球公开赛和温网。

paragraphs.en: Overwork is a perverse consequence of success: The more you win, the more matches you play, the more your joints get pulverized—and the more prize money you make. There’s an uneasy synergy in tennis between pain and profit. Although Alcaraz made it sound like he was powerless to resist the unremitting tennis calendar, players are able to opt out. In 2023, the 21-year-old Amanda Anisimova announced on Instagram that her life on tour had “become unbearable,” and that she was stepping away to prioritize her mental health.
paragraphs.zh: 过度劳累是成功带来的反常结果：你赢得越多，打的比赛越多，关节被“碾碎”的程度越重，而赚得的奖金也越多。网球里，痛苦与收益之间总有一种微妙而不安的联动。尽管阿尔卡拉斯听起来像是无力摆脱不间断赛程的安排，实际上球员是可以选择退出的。2023年，21岁的阿曼达·阿尼西莫娃在 Instagram 上宣布，巡回赛生活“变得令人无法承受”，并表示她要暂时离开以优先照顾自己的心理健康。

paragraphs.en: Anisimova, who’d been tapped as a can’t-miss star when she made the French Open semifinals at 17, threw herself into painting and worked with a therapist who specialized in trauma. She came back to tennis after eight months away, and with the help of a Swedish Canadian physiotherapist—Futterman calls her “the chief operating officer of Anisimova’s body”—transformed from an injury-riddled athlete into a strong, confident title contender. Last year, she made the finals of both Wimbledon and the U.S. Open.
paragraphs.zh: 阿尼西莫娃在17岁打进法网四强时，被视为“必然会成为明星”的球员。她把自己投入到绘画中，并与一位专门治疗创伤的治疗师合作。她离开赛场八个月后重返网球，并在一位瑞典裔加拿大理疗师的帮助下复出——福特曼把这位理疗师称作“阿尼西莫娃身体的首席运营官”——她也从一个伤病缠身的运动员转变为一名强健、更加自信的夺冠竞争者。去年，她闯入了温网和美国公开赛两个决赛。

paragraphs.en: Although male players have become more open about their mental-health struggles, the sport’s leading men rarely go on leave. “There were months when I thought about taking a complete break from tennis to cleanse my mind,” Nadal said in a post-retirement essay for The Players’ Tribune. “I conquered it by always moving forward.” For cultural and biological reasons, female tennis players far more commonly take sabbaticals. Maja Chwalińska, a surprise finalist at this year’s French Open, took a hiatus in 2021 after, she said, the sport became a source of “pressure, stress, and crying.” Williams, Osaka, and Elina Svitolina are among the many women who have returned to the tour after giving birth. (Williams, who “evolved away” from tennis as she prepared to have her second child, has now launched another comeback, at age 44.) That path is slightly less arduous now that players have succeeded in pushing the WTA Tour to make accommodations for women who want to start families—paid leave, grants for fertility treatments, ranking protection during time off.
paragraphs.zh: 虽然男球员在心理健康困境上的表达更为公开，但这项运动的顶级男运动员很少请假。纳达尔在《球员论坛》的退役文章中说：“我有几个月想到要给自己一个彻底的离场，来清理一下大脑。”他补充道：“我靠着始终向前走把它克服了。”出于文化和生理原因，女网球运动员更常选择休整。Maja Chwalińska，这位今年法网的黑马决赛选手，在2021年就曾暂停职业生涯，她说自己当时离开是因为这项运动成了“压力、紧张和哭泣”的来源。威廉姆斯、大坂，以及Elina Svitolina都在产后重返巡回赛的名单中。（威廉姆斯在准备第二胎时“慢慢远离”了网球，如今已44岁再度发起下一次复出。）如今这条路略显不那么艰难，因为球员们已成功推动 WTA 巡回赛为想要成家的女性球员提供了配套：带薪休假、助孕治疗补助、休赛期间的排名保护。

paragraphs.en: Women have been battling for just policies since the creation of modern pro tennis in the late 1960s. Billie Jean King and eight others fought against pay disparities by forming their own tour. Thanks to the efforts of King and Venus Williams, who’s still competing at 46, female players as of 2007 receive prize money equal to men’s at all four Grand Slams. Now they’re pushing to get even bigger checks, for themselves and their male peers. At a press conference in May, Sabalenka raised the prospect of a Grand Slam boycott if players don’t get a larger portion of tournament revenue. Gauff backed her up, saying, “If everyone were to move as one and collaborate, then yeah, I can 100 percent see that.”
paragraphs.zh: 自上世纪60年代末现代职业网球诞生以来，女性就一直在为争取这些政策而奋斗。比莉·珍·金与另外八位球员联合发起，反对薪酬差距并建立了自己的巡回赛。得益于金和仍在46岁继续征战的维纳斯·威廉姆斯等人的努力，到了2007年，女球员在四大满贯中的奖金与男球员实现了同额。如今，她们正在推动更大的奖金改进，不仅为自己，也包括她们的男性同行。在5月的一次记者会上，萨巴伦卡提出，如果赛事收入分配不给球员更多份额，可能会出现大满贯赛事集体抵制。加乌夫表示支持：“如果每个人都能统一行动并协作，那么，完全可以——我百分之百能理解并支持。”

paragraphs.en: I can’t quite picture players walking out of a Grand Slam, and tennis’s lack of a formally recognized players’ union has historically made collective action difficult. But that could be changing: Sabalenka, Gauff, Świątek, Sinner, and other highly ranked men and women came together at the French Open, cutting short their initial media obligations as part of their protest over prize money. The balkanization of tennis could also be a source of leverage. The men’s and women’s tours are distinct entities, as are the sport’s four major tournaments. If one of the tours cuts down on the number of mandatory events, or one of the Grand Slams ratchets up its payouts, the others might feel compelled to follow suit.
paragraphs.zh: 我很难想象选手在大满贯赛事结束后走下场，过去网球缺乏一个正式承认的职业球员协会，也使得集体行动一直很困难。但这种状况可能正在改变：萨巴伦卡、加夫、斯瓦泰克、辛纳以及其他高排名的男女选手，在法国公开赛期间聚在一起，就奖金问题发起抗议，并缩短了最初的媒体行程。网球的“分裂化”也可能成为一种筹码。男子和女子职业巡回赛本就是两个独立体系，四个大满贯赛事也彼此分离。如果其中一个巡回赛减少强制参赛赛事数量，或其中一站大满贯提高奖金发放，其他组织可能就会被迫跟进。

paragraphs.en: It’s also possible that, as King did more than half a century ago, the players could create something new, in partnership with or in opposition to the existing tour and Grand Slam leadership. Rumors have circulated for years about a combined “Premier Tour” for the sport’s top tier, with fewer events and a real offseason. Part of tennis’s appeal comes from its connection to history—the fact that players have been stepping onto the grass at Wimbledon since the 1800s. The new elite have shown no sign that they want to abandon tradition, but they also won’t be cowed by it. Federer, Nadal, and Williams left an unmatched tennis legacy. This generation could reshape the entire sport.
paragraphs.zh: 也完全有可能像五十多年以前King所做的那样，选手们与现有的巡回赛和大满贯领导层合作，或对其进行对抗，创造出一种全新的模式。多年来，关于一项面向顶级选手、赛事更少且真正拥有休赛期的“超级巡回赛”合并方案，一直有各种传闻。网球的一大吸引力来自其历史联系——球员自1800年代以来就在温网草地上登场。新一代球员并没有显示出想要抛弃传统的迹象，但他们也不会因传统而退缩。费德勒、纳达尔和威廉姆斯留下了无可匹敌的网球传奇。这个世代可能会重塑整项运动。

paragraphs.en: This article appears in the August 2026 print edition with the headline “Tennis’s New Golden Age.”
paragraphs.zh: 这篇文章刊登在2026年8月纸质版中的标题是“网球的新黄金时代”。
